title: tilia rubra subsp. caucasica
intro: Tilia rubra subsp. caucasica, also known as Caucasian small-leaved linden, is a subspecies of deciduous tree native to the Caucasus region. The bark and leaves of this plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and sedative properties, making it a popular remedy for treating anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Tilia rubra subsp. caucasica include reducing stress and promoting relaxation, improving sleep quality, and alleviating symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome. Herbal preparations of this plant are often made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
